description: Declarative mode registry: the single source of truth for the discriminator (workflowMode/packetKind/backendKind) PLUS the advisorRouting projection for mcp-tooling. The hub reads this file to resolve a mode; the advisor routes any MCP tool-bridge query to the single identity mcp-tooling and the hub picks the mode. All modes route by hub membership (routingClass 'metadata'), so there is no advisor map entry per mode and no merged-identity projection — the advisor does not read this file at runtime.

discriminator.workflowMode: Public hub/mode key. Stable identity used by the hub router and this registry. Carried by every mode.
discriminator.packetKind: The axis discriminator: 'workflow' for the local-mutating tool bridges (mcp-chrome-devtools, mcp-click-up, mcp-aside-devtools) that write to this repo's workspace; 'transport' for mcp-figma, which bridges to Figma Desktop rather than performing design judgment or mutating this workspace.
discriminator.backendKind: Which backend runs the mode: cli-plus-mcp for the workflow bridges (CLI-primary, Code Mode MCP fallback); figma-desktop-transport for the figma transport (drives the external Figma Desktop app over its local daemon); code-mode-remote-mcp for the refero and mobbin transports (remote MCP servers reached through Code Mode via their registered manuals).

advisorRoutingContract.routingClass: How the advisor finds the mode. For mcp-tooling every mode is 'metadata' = resolved by hub membership; the advisor routes the single identity mcp-tooling and the hub picks the mode, so no mode carries an advisor map entry or a merged-identity projection.
advisorRoutingContract.packetSkillName: The packet's SKILL.md name. For mcp-tooling the packet folder IS its packetSkillName for every mode — so no mode has a folder/name mismatch and grandfatheredFolderMismatch is false throughout.
advisorRoutingContract.grandfatheredFolderMismatch: true only when a packet folder differs from its packetSkillName (a preserved legacy mismatch). All mcp-tooling modes use folder == packetSkillName, so none has a mismatch — grandfatheredFolderMismatch is false for all.
advisorRoutingContract.command: The bound slash command for the mode. No mcp-tooling mode has a dedicated command today (command:null on all, per ADR-006); the /doctor:mcp router is a separate command that covers install/debug for every mcp-* skill, including mcp-tooling's members.

transport-axis.description: Declares the TRANSPORT axis. packetKind:'transport' entries bridge to an external tool's CLI/MCP surface (routingClass:'metadata'). mutatesWorkspace:false declares the DOMINANT mutation posture: document/content mutation lands in the external tool, and no transport edits existing repo files (Write/Edit/Task forbidden). Local write nuance is declared per mode via an optional toolSurface.workspaceWrites clarifier: mcp-figma is export-only (its export commands save requested artifacts to explicit local output paths); mcp-refero and mcp-mobbin perform no local writes at all. Transports never perform design judgment themselves and carry a mandatory cross-hub pairing to sk-design (a DIFFERENT hub) for design decisions — licensed by ADR-002 as a legitimate cross-hub judgment partner, since none of mcp-tooling's own workflow modes (browser inspection, task management, agentic browser automation) can supply design judgment. backendKinds: figma-desktop-transport (mcp-figma, local Figma Desktop daemon); code-mode-remote-mcp (mcp-refero via the registered refero manual; mcp-mobbin via the registered mobbin manual — both remote MCP through Code Mode).

toolSurface.workspaceWrites: export-only: the read-path export commands (extract DESIGN.md, export PNG/SVG/JSX/CSS/Tailwind/Storybook, screenshots) save their OUTPUT ARTIFACTS to the local filesystem at an explicit, operator-supplied output path (never a silent overwrite, per the packet's local-export rule). No command edits existing repo sources; document mutation lands in Figma Desktop, so mutatesWorkspace stays false per the transport axis' dominant-posture semantics.
